title: Climate Classification Changes
text:
Introduction
This project analyzes global climate classification changes by comparing climate data from a recent decadal period (2015–2024) with the WMO Climatological Reference Period (1961–1990).
The analysis will be done by applying the Köppen-Trewartha climate classification system to downloaded ERA5 climate data, identifying and visualizing the climate classification changes.

title: Climate Classification Changes
text:
Process
This project uses a multi-step process:

Data Acquisition


Download and save ERA5 climate reanalysis gridded data.


Climate Aggregation


Aggregate multi-year monthly data into climatological averages.


Determinant Calculation


Calculate the classification determinants required by the Köppen–Trewartha system.


Climate Classification


Apply the Köppen–Trewartha classification system to assign climate groups and types.


Change Analysis


Identify and visualize the shifts in classification between the climate periods.

title: Climate Classification Changes
text:
Methods at a glance

Data & periods. ERA5 monthly temperature & precipitation at the native grid. Two periods: CRP (1961–1990) and DCP (2015–2024). Analyses are land-only (lsm > 0) and area-weighted by grid-cell area (area).
Determinants.
Temperature: tavg (mean of 12 months), tmax/tmin (row-wise max/min), tg10 (months ≥ 10 °C).
Precipitation: ptot (annual total), pwnt (winter total), psmr (summer total), pmin, pl60 (months < 60 mm).
Aridity threshold (mm): [(2.3 × tavg − 0.64 × winter% + 41) × 10], where winter% = 100 × (pwnt / ptot).
Seasons follow hemisphere: NH winter = Jan–Mar & Oct–Dec; SH winter = Apr–Sep.
Summaries & visuals. Area-weighted tables (CRP, DCP, Δ) and figures: temperature distributions, seasonal composition (winter vs summer), tropical moisture (pl60), and aridity threshold with dry/not-dry split.
Classification & change. Köppen–Trewartha rules are applied per grid cell downstream; we compare CRP → DCP class changes and report area and share deltas.







Note



Quick links - Determinant Calculation → 03_determinant_calculation.html
- Change Analysis → 05_change_analysis.html

title: 1  Data Acquisition
text:
1.2 Functions
Function to download ERA5 temperature and precipitation data.


Code
def fn_download_climate_data(
    pth: str, 
    var: str,
    beg: int,
    end: int,
    log_df: pd.DataFrame):

    start_time = datetime.now()

    out = Path(pth)
    yrs = list(range(beg, end+1))
    mths = list(range(1, 13))

    if var == "t":
        variable_name = "2m_temperature"
    elif var == "p":
        variable_name = "total_precipitation"

    c = cdsapi.Client()

    c.retrieve(
        "reanalysis-era5-single-levels-monthly-means",
        {
            "product_type": "monthly_averaged_reanalysis",
            "variable": variable_name,
            "year": yrs,
            "month": mths,
            "time": "00:00",
            "format": "netcdf"
        },
        str(out),
    )

    file_name = os.path.basename(pth)
    size_mb = round(os.path.getsize(pth) / (1024 * 1024),2)

    end_time = datetime.now()  
    elapsed_time = end_time - start_time  
    total_seconds = int(elapsed_time.total_seconds())
    mm, ss = divmod(total_seconds, 60)
    elapsed_mmss = f"{mm:02d}:{ss:02d}"

    # Append new row to log file
    new_row = {
        "file_name": file_name,
        "variable_name": variable_name,
        "file_size": size_mb,
        "elapsed_mmss": elapsed_mmss
    }

    log_df = pd.concat([log_df, pd.DataFrame([new_row])], ignore_index=True)

    return(log_df)


Function to download ERA5 land sea mask.


Code
def fn_download_land_sea_mask(
    pth: str,
    log_df: pd.DataFrame):

    start_time = datetime.now()

    out = Path(pth)
    variable_name = "land_sea_mask"

    c = cdsapi.Client() 
    c.retrieve(
        "reanalysis-era5-single-levels",
        {
            "product_type": "reanalysis",
            "variable": variable_name,
            "year": "2000",
            "month": "01",
            "day": "01",
            "time": "00:00",
            "area": "90/-179.75/-90/180", # N/W/S/E (global)
            "grid": "0.25/0.25", # ERA5 native grid spacing
            "format": "netcdf"
        },
        str(out),
    )
    
    file_name = os.path.basename(pth)
    size_mb = round(os.path.getsize(pth) / (1024 * 1024),2)

    end_time = datetime.now()  
    elapsed_time = end_time - start_time  
    total_seconds = int(elapsed_time.total_seconds())
    mm, ss = divmod(total_seconds, 60)
    elapsed_mmss = f"{mm:02d}:{ss:02d}"

    # Append new row to log file
    new_row = {
        "file_name": file_name,
        "variable_name": variable_name,
        "file_size": size_mb,
        "elapsed_mmss": elapsed_mmss
    }

    log_df = pd.concat([log_df, pd.DataFrame([new_row])], ignore_index=True)

    return(log_df)

title: 2  Climate Aggregation
text:
2.1 Initialization
This code processes ERA5 climate data (temperature and precipitation) by aggregating it monthly, transforming units, pivoting to wide format, adding land-sea masks and grid cell areas. It then reshapes data from long form to wide form: one row per grid cell with each monthly value in a separate column. This will facilitate the calculation of climate classification determinants.
The aggregation process is done using Python.
It will use packages:

title: 2  Climate Aggregation
text:
2.2 Subroutines

2.2.1 Import Data


Code
def sr_import_nc(fil: str, vnm: str, log_df: pd.DataFrame) -> [xr.Dataset, pd.DataFrame]:
    pth = Path(idir) / fil
    ds = xr.open_dataset(pth, engine="h5netcdf", chunks="auto", decode_cf=True)
    cnt_months = ds.sizes["valid_time"]
    cnt_lat = ds.sizes["latitude"]
    cnt_lon = ds.sizes["longitude"]
    cnt_values = ds[vnm].size
    new_row = {
        "file_name": fil,
        "variable_name": vnm,
        "latitude_count": cnt_lat,
        "longitude_count": cnt_lon,
        "input_month_count": cnt_months,
        "input_values_count": cnt_values,
        "agg_month_count": 12,
        "agg_values_count": cnt_lat * cnt_lon * 12
        }
    log_df = pd.concat([log_df, pd.DataFrame([new_row])], ignore_index=True)
    return ds, log_df




2.2.2 Transform Data


Code
def sr_transform_data(ds: xr.Dataset, cv: str, vnm: str) -> pd.DataFrame:
    # Drop unused variables
    ds = ds.drop_vars("number", errors="ignore")
    # Rename coordinates
    ds = ds.rename({"longitude": "lon", "latitude": "lat"})
    # Convert longitude from 0–360 to -180–180
    ds["lon"] = xr.where(ds["lon"] > 180, ds["lon"] - 360, ds["lon"])
    ds["lon"] = ds["lon"].astype("float32")
    ds["lat"] = ds["lat"].astype("float32")

    # Assign month coordinate
    ds = ds.assign_coords(mth=ds["valid_time"].dt.month)
    ds["mth"] = ds["mth"].astype("int8")

    # Group by month and compute mean
    grp_vnm_da = ds[vnm].groupby("mth").mean("valid_time", keep_attrs=True)   

    # Convert data array to dataframe 
    df = grp_vnm_da.to_dataframe().reset_index()

    #df = df.astype({"mth": "int8"})

    if cv == "t":
        df["t"] = df["t2m"] - 273.15  # convert temperature from kelvin to celsius
        df = df[["lon", "lat", "mth", "t"]]  # select columns to keep
   
    elif cv == "p":
        # precipitation is stored as a daily average, need to multiply by days in month
        
        # climate period average number of days in each month number (12 values)
        ds["ndy"] = ds["valid_time"].dt.days_in_month
        da_ndy = ds["ndy"].groupby("mth").mean("valid_time", keep_attrs=True)     
        df_ndy = da_ndy.to_dataframe().reset_index() # convert to df before merging

        # merge precipation df with ndy
        df = df.merge(df_ndy[["mth", "ndy"]], on="mth", how="left")

        # conversion from daily avg in m to monthly avg in mm
        df["p"] = df["tp"] * df["ndy"] * 1000
       
        df = df[["lon", "lat", "mth", "p"]]  # select columns to keep

    return df




2.2.3 Pivot Data


Code
def sr_pivot_data(df: pd.DataFrame, cv: str) -> pd.DataFrame:
    # Pivot to wide form
    wide_df = df.pivot(index=["lon", "lat"], columns="mth", values=cv)
    # Ensure columns are in month order 1–12
    wide_df = wide_df.reindex(columns=range(1, 13))
    # Rename columns 1..12 to m1..m12
    wide_df.columns = [f"m{m}" for m in wide_df.columns]
    # Reset index to keep lon and lat as normal columns
    wide_df = wide_df.reset_index()
    # merge with land sea mask based on lon and lat
    wide_df = wide_df.merge(df_lsm, on=["lon", "lat"],  how="left")
    return(wide_df)




2.2.4 Calculate Area


Code
def sr_calculate_area(df: pd.DataFrame) -> pd.DataFrame:
    earth_radius_km = 6371 # km
    lat_grid_degrees = 0.25
    lon_grid_degrees = 0.25
    # convert degrees to radians
    lat_radians = np.radians(df["lat"])
    lat_grid_radians = np.radians(lat_grid_degrees)
    lon_grid_radians = np.radians(lon_grid_degrees)
    # cell area in million km²
    df = df.assign(
        area=(earth_radius_km**2 *
              lon_grid_radians *
              (np.sin(lat_radians + lat_grid_radians / 2) -
               np.sin(lat_radians - lat_grid_radians / 2))) / 1e6
    )
    # reorder columns: lat, lon, area, lsm, then everything else
    cols = ["lat", "lon", "area", "lsm"]
    other_cols = [c for c in df.columns if c not in cols]
    df = df[cols + other_cols]
    return df




2.2.5 Save


Code
def sr_save(df: pd.DataFrame, fil: str):
    pth = Path(odir) / fil
    df.to_parquet(pth, index=False)

title: 2  Climate Aggregation
text:
2.3 Main Process


Code
def process_dataset(ifil: str, ofil: str, cv: str, vnm: str,
 log_df:pd.DataFrame) -> (pd.DataFrame, pd.DataFrame):
    ds, log_df = sr_import_nc(ifil, vnm, log_df)
    transform_df = sr_transform_data(ds, cv, vnm)
    pivot_df = sr_pivot_data(transform_df, cv)
    area_df = sr_calculate_area(pivot_df)
    sr_save(area_df, ofil)   
    return log_df

# Main process
start = datetime.now()
log_df = process_dataset(ifil1, ofil1, "t", "t2m", log_df)
log_df = process_dataset(ifil2, ofil2, "p", "tp", log_df)
log_df = process_dataset(ifil3, ofil3, "t", "t2m", log_df)
log_df = process_dataset(ifil4, ofil4, "p", "tp", log_df)

end = datetime.now()
print("elapsed time: " + str(end - start))

title: Determinant Calculation
text:
Initialization
Calculate climate classification determinants (temperature, precipitation, aridity) for two periods and summarize/visualize area-weighted results. These feed a later step that applies Köppen–Trewartha classification.
Determinants computed
The classification definitions are based on
https://scispace.com/pdf/climate-classification-revisited-from-koppen-to-trewartha-40ofumhxzu.pdf

Code
# Period labeling
pretty_period_lbl <- function(x) recode(x, CRP = CRP_LBL, DCP = DCP_LBL)

# Area-weighted average for a set of metrics for CRP/DCP lists
fn_weighted_average <- function(df, metrics) {
  df |>
    select(area, any_of(metrics)) |>
    pivot_longer(-area, names_to = "metric", values_to = "value") |>
    group_by(metric) |>
    summarise(wgtavg = weighted.mean(value, w = area, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")
}

wgt_by_metric <- function(det_list, metrics) {
  bind_rows(
    fn_weighted_average(det_list$crp, metrics) |> mutate(period = "CRP"),
    fn_weighted_average(det_list$dcp, metrics) |> mutate(period = "DCP")
  ) |>
    pivot_wider(names_from = period, values_from = wgtavg) |>
    rename(crp_metric = CRP, dcp_metric = DCP) |>
    mutate(metric_chg = dcp_metric - crp_metric)
}

# Long-to-tidy two-period prep
prepare_period_df <- function(df_crp, df_dcp, vars) {
  df_crp |>
    select(lon, lat, area, all_of(vars)) |>
    left_join(
      df_dcp |> select(lon, lat, area, all_of(vars)),
      by = c("lon", "lat") # safer join; area should match but don't join on it
    ) |>
    pivot_longer(
      cols = ends_with(c(".x", ".y")),
      names_to = c(".value", "period_side"),
      names_sep = "\\."
    ) |>
    transmute(
      lon, lat, area,
      across(all_of(vars)),
      period = if_else(period_side == "x", "CRP", "DCP"),
      period_lbl = factor(pretty_period_lbl(period),levels = c(CRP_LBL, DCP_LBL))
    )
}

# Reusable gt table helper for CRP/DCP + Change
gt_det_table <- function(df, title, subtitle, decimals = 2) {
  df %>%
    gt(rowname_col = "metric") %>%
    tab_header(title = title, subtitle = subtitle) %>%
    cols_label(crp_metric = "CRP", dcp_metric = "DCP", metric_chg = "Change") %>%
    fmt_number(columns = c(crp_metric, dcp_metric, metric_chg), decimals = decimals) %>%
    tab_style(style = cell_text(align = "left"),  locations = cells_stub()) %>%
    tab_style(style = cell_text(v_align = "middle"), locations = cells_body(rows = everything())) %>%
    tab_style(style = cell_text(align = "right"),
              locations = cells_body(columns = c(crp_metric, dcp_metric, metric_chg))) %>%
    tab_style(style = cell_text(align = "center"),
              locations = cells_column_labels(columns = c(crp_metric, dcp_metric, metric_chg)))
}

title: Determinant Calculation
text:
Calculate Determinants
Temperature determinants (CRP vs DCP). Area‑weighted averages for tavg, tmax, tmin, and months > 10 °C (TG10). Shifts indicate warming/cooling and potential movement across Köppen–Trewartha thermal thresholds (10 °C, 18 °C).


Code
calculate_t_determinants <- function(df) {
  mcols <- paste0("m", 1:12)
  df %>%
    filter(lsm > 0) %>%
    mutate(
      # average across 12 monthly columns
      tavg = rowMeans(across(all_of(mcols)), na.rm = TRUE),
      # row-wise max/min via do.call on the across() columns
      tmax = do.call(pmax, c(across(all_of(mcols)), na.rm = TRUE)),
      tmin = do.call(pmin, c(across(all_of(mcols)), na.rm = TRUE)),
      # count months >= 10 °C
      tg10 = rowSums(across(all_of(mcols), ~ .x >= 10), na.rm = TRUE)
    ) %>%
    select(-all_of(mcols))
}

t_det <- map(t_import, calculate_t_determinants)

df_wgtavg_temp <- wgt_by_metric(t_det, temp_metrics) |>
  mutate(metric = recode(metric,
    tavg = "Temperature: Avg",
    tmax = "Temperature: Max",
    tmin = "Temperature: Min",
    tg10 = "Temperature: Months > 10°C"
  )) |>
  arrange(factor(metric, c(
    "Temperature: Avg","Temperature: Max","Temperature: Min","Temperature: Months > 10°C"
  )))

gt_tbl_temp <- gt_det_table(
  df_wgtavg_temp,
  title    = "Temperature Determinants Summary",
  subtitle = "Area-Weighted Averages (°C)",
  decimals = 2
)

Precipitation determinants (CRP vs DCP). Area‑weighted averages for annual total (ptot), winter/summer share (pwnt/psmr), driest month (pmin), and months < 60 mm (PL60). Changes influence dry/wet regimes and tropical subtype splits.


Code
calculate_p_determinants <- function(df) {
  mcols <- paste0("m", 1:12)
  df %>%
    filter(lsm > 0) %>%
    mutate(
      ptot = rowSums(across(all_of(mcols)), na.rm = TRUE),
      pmin = do.call(pmin, c(across(all_of(mcols)), na.rm = TRUE)),
      pl60 = rowSums(across(all_of(mcols), ~ .x < 60), na.rm = TRUE),
      pwnt = if_else(
        lat >= 0,
        rowSums(across(all_of(NORTH_WINTER)), na.rm = TRUE),
        rowSums(across(all_of(NORTH_SUMMER)), na.rm = TRUE)
      ),
      psmr = if_else(
        lat >= 0,
        rowSums(across(all_of(NORTH_SUMMER)), na.rm = TRUE),
        rowSums(across(all_of(NORTH_WINTER)), na.rm = TRUE)
      )
    ) %>%
    select(-all_of(mcols))
}

p_det <- map(p_import, calculate_p_determinants)

df_wgtavg_precip <- wgt_by_metric(p_det, precip_metrics) |>
  mutate(metric = recode(metric,
    ptot = "Precipitation: Total",
    pmin = "Precipitation: Min",
    pl60 = "Precipitation: Months < 60 mm",
    pwnt = "Precipitation: Winter",
    psmr = "Precipitation: Summer"
  )) |>
  arrange(factor(metric, c(
    "Precipitation: Total",
    "Precipitation: Winter",
    "Precipitation: Summer",
    "Precipitation: Min",
    "Precipitation: Months < 60 mm"
  )))

Aridity components and threshold. Decomposes the aridity formula (2.3 × tavg) + (−0.64 × winter %) + 41 into terms and the resulting threshold (athr). A higher athr increases the precipation needed to not be classified as dry.


Code
calculate_aridity_threshold <- function(df) {
  df %>%
    mutate(
      pw_pct = if_else(ptot != 0, (pwnt / ptot) * 100, 0),
      athr   = (2.3 * tavg - 0.64 * pw_pct + 41) * 10  # cm to mm -> mm
    ) %>%
    select(-pw_pct)
}

# Build aridity inputs with a safe join and unified columns
a_det <- map2(
  t_det, p_det,
  ~ left_join(.x, .y, by = c("lon","lat"), suffix = c(".t",".p")) %>%
      mutate(
        # unify duplicates from temperature (.t) and precipitation (.p)
        area = coalesce(area.t, area.p),
        lsm  = coalesce(lsm.t,  lsm.p)
      ) %>%
      select(-ends_with(".t"), -ends_with(".p")) %>%
      calculate_aridity_threshold()
)

# Robust weighted-term calculator (ensures x and w have same length)
calc_weighted_term <- function(df, term_name, expr) {
  df %>%
    transmute(area, value = {{ expr }}) %>%
    filter(is.finite(area), area > 0, is.finite(value)) %>%
    summarise(wgtavg = weighted.mean(value, w = area, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
    mutate(metric = term_name) %>%
    select(metric, wgtavg)
}

# Compute weighted averages for the three terms in each period
df_wgtavg_aridity <- map2_df(
  rep(list(a_det$crp, a_det$dcp), each = 3),
  rep(c("tavg_term1", "pwnt_ptot", "athr"), 2),
  ~ calc_weighted_term(
      .x,
      .y,
      switch(.y,
        tavg_term1 = tavg * 2.3,
        pwnt_ptot  = if_else(ptot != 0, ((pwnt / ptot) * 100 * -0.64) + 41, 41),
        athr       = athr
      )
    )
) %>%
  mutate(period = rep(c("crp", "dcp"), each = 3)) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = period, values_from = wgtavg, names_prefix = "wgtavg_") %>%
  rename(crp_metric = wgtavg_crp, dcp_metric = wgtavg_dcp) %>%
  mutate(
    metric_chg = dcp_metric - crp_metric,
    metric = recode(metric,
      tavg_term1 = "Term1: Annual Average Temperature * 2.3",
      pwnt_ptot  = "Term2: (% of Precipitation in Winter) * -0.64 + 41",
      athr       = "Aridity Threshold: (Term1 + Term2) * 10"
    ),
    metric = factor(metric, levels = c(
      "Term1: Annual Average Temperature * 2.3",
      "Term2: (% of Precipitation in Winter) * -0.64 + 41",
      "Aridity Threshold: (Term1 + Term2) * 10"
    ))
  ) %>%
  arrange(metric)


gt_tbl_aridity <- gt_det_table(
  df_wgtavg_aridity,
  title    = "Aridity Threshold Summary",
  subtitle = "Area-Weighted Averages (mm)",
  decimals = 2
)

Annual precipitation distribution (log10). Comparing CRP vs DCP reveals whether total precipitation shifted. Lower values push more cells toward dryness in the aridity test.


Code
df_ptot <- prepare_period_df(a_det$crp, a_det$dcp, c("ptot")) %>%
filter(is.finite(ptot), ptot > 0, area > 0) %>%
mutate(period = factor(period, levels = c("CRP","DCP")),
period_lbl = factor(period_lbl, levels = c(CRP_LBL, DCP_LBL)))

ggplot(df_ptot, aes(x = ptot, weight = area, fill = period)) +
    geom_density(adjust = 1, alpha = 1.0, color = NA) +
    scale_x_log10(labels = label_number(scale_cut = cut_short_scale())) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = role_palette$period) +

      title = "Annual Total Precipitation",
      subtitle = "Identifies Dry Climates when < Aridity Threshold",
      x = "mm (log10)",
      y = "Area-weighted density") +

    theme(legend.position = "none")











Code
# Bring CRP/DCP determinants and compute seasonal proportions per grid cell
df <- prepare_period_df(a_det$crp, a_det$dcp, c("pwnt","psmr","ptot")) |>
  filter(is.finite(ptot), is.finite(pwnt), is.finite(psmr), area > 0, ptot > 0) |>
  mutate(
    winter_prop = pwnt / ptot,
    summer_prop = psmr / ptot
  )

# Area-weighted mean proportions by period
agg_pct <- df |>
  group_by(period_lbl) |>
  summarise(
    Winter = weighted.mean(winter_prop, w = area, na.rm = TRUE),
    Summer = weighted.mean(summer_prop, w = area, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  tidyr::pivot_longer(cols = c(Winter, Summer),
                      names_to = "season", values_to = "prop") |>
  mutate(
    season = factor(season, levels = c("Winter","Summer")),
    period_lbl = factor(period_lbl, levels = c(CRP_LBL, DCP_LBL))
  )

# Compute a compact upper limit: +15% headroom, cap at 100%, round up to nearest 5%
y_max <- min(1, max(agg_pct$prop, na.rm = TRUE) * 1.15)
y_max <- 0.05 * ceiling(y_max / 0.05)
if (!is.finite(y_max) || y_max <= 0) y_max <- 0.05

fill_vals <- role_palette$season

# Side-by-side bars with percent y-axis
p_seasonal <- ggplot(agg_pct, aes(x = season, y = prop, fill = season)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.6, color = "gray20") +
  facet_wrap(~ period_lbl, ncol = 2) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = fill_vals) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1),
    limits = c(0, y_max),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.03))
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Seasonal Percentage of Total Annual Precipitation",
    subtitle = "Used in Aridity Threshold Calculation",
    x = NULL, y = "Share of Annual Precipitation (%)"
  ) +
  theme(strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"), legend.position = "none")

  filter(is.finite(athr), athr > 0, area > 0) %>%
  mutate(

# Plot aridity threshold

  ggplot(aes(x = athr, weight = area, fill = dry_status)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 50, boundary = 0, color = "white") +

pt_size <- 0.25
pt_alpha <- 0.15

ar_scatter <- prepare_period_df(a_det$crp, a_det$dcp, c("athr", "ptot", "tmax")) %>%
  filter(is.finite(ptot), is.finite(athr), ptot > 0, athr > 0, tmax > 10, area > 0) %>%
  mutate(
    period = pretty_period_lbl(period),

ggplot(ar_scatter, aes(x = athr, y = ptot, color = dry_status)) +
  # keep the boundary line out of the legend + give it a neutral color
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed",
              color = "grey50", inherit.aes = FALSE) +

  ) +

  guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(alpha = 1, size = 3))) +

pth <- paste(climate_classification_dir, "ktc_grp_classification.gpkg", sep = "")
import_sf <- st_read(pth, quiet=TRUE)

import_sf <- import_sf |>  
  mutate(cgrp = factor(cgrp, levels = grp_levels))
import_sf <- import_sf |>  
  mutate(rgrp = factor(rgrp, levels = grp_levels))
import_df <- import_sf |> 
  st_drop_geometry() 

# remove rows where rgrp and cgrp are the same
shifts_sf <- import_sf |> filter(cgrp != rgrp)
shifts_df <- import_df |> filter(cgrp != rgrp)

title: 5  Change Analysis
5  Change Analysis
text:
5.9 Analysis

5.9.1 Summary

5.9.1.1 Summary Table


Code
# summmarize crp group mkm and pct
df1 <- import_df |>
  summarize(
    mkm_c = sum(mkm),
    pct_c = sum(pct),
    .by = c(cgrp)) |>
  rename(cg = cgrp)

# summmarize dcp group mkm and pct
df2 <- import_df |>
  summarize(
    mkm_r = sum(mkm),
    pct_r = sum(pct),
    .by = c(rgrp)) |>
  rename(cg = rgrp)

# join crp and dcp and calculate difference
join_df <- df1 |> 
  inner_join(df2, by = "cg") |>
  mutate(mkm_d = mkm_r - mkm_c) |>
  mutate(pct_d = pct_r - pct_c)

# shifts from other groups
df3 <- shifts_df |>
  summarize(
    mkm_g = sum(mkm),
    pct_g = sum(pct),
    .by = c(rgrp)) |>
  rename(cg = rgrp)

# join tables
join_df <- join_df  |> 
  inner_join(df3, by = "cg")

# shifts to other groups
df4 <- shifts_df |>
  summarize(
    mkm_l = sum(mkm) * -1,
    pct_l = sum(pct) * -1,
    .by = c(cgrp)) |>
  rename(cg = cgrp)

# join tables
join_df <- join_df  |> 
    inner_join(df4, by = "cg")
    
# select columns for table to display    
tbl_df <- join_df  |> 
    select(cg,
      mkm_c, mkm_g, mkm_l, mkm_r, mkm_d,
      pct_c, pct_g, pct_l, pct_r, pct_d
    ) |> 
    mutate(across(c(pct_c, pct_g, pct_l, pct_r, pct_d), ~ .x / 100))

# create gt table
t1 <- fn_build_summary_table(tbl_df)

5.9.2.2 Detail Analysis


Code
fn_detail_analysis <- function(shifts_df, shifts_sf, grp_palette, g) {

  tbl_df <- shifts_df |> 
  filter(rgrp == g) |>
  mutate(pct = pct / 100) |>
  arrange(rgrp)
  t1 <- fn_build_detail_table(tbl_df, paste("New Group ", g))

  g_new_sf <- shifts_sf |> 
  filter(rgrp == g) |>
  select(rgrp) |>
  rename(grp = rgrp)

  p1 <- fn_build_global_map(g_new_sf, grp_palette, "dcp")

  g_old_sf <- shifts_sf |> 
  filter(rgrp == g) |>
  select(cgrp) |>
  rename(grp = cgrp)

  p2 <- fn_build_global_map(g_old_sf, grp_palette, "crp")

 return(list(t1 = t1, p1 = p1, p2 = p2))

}

text:
Import Data

import_data <- function(type, period_name) {
  beg <- periods[[period_name]]$beg
  end <- periods[[period_name]]$end
  fil <- paste0(type, "_agg_", beg, "-", end, ".parquet")
  read_parquet(file.path(idir, fil))
}
t_import <- list(crp = import_data("t", "crp"),
                 dcp = import_data("t", "dcp"))
p_import <- list(crp = import_data("p", "crp"),
                 dcp = import_data("p", "dcp"))
